channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: PlayStation Announces Ending Physical Disc Production For All New Games in 2028
item.content:encoded:
It was only last week that there was controversy over the fact that Grant Theft Auto VI was releasing with only digital code in case but that seems passe in the wake of Sony's bombshell announcement on this Canada Day - starting in January 2028 physical game production for all new games releasing on PlayStation consoles will be digital formats only.

This will apply to all new games, not just first party titles, and Sony claims that it is "adapt to consumer trends as the general preference for digital media significantly outpaces physical discs". For games that release prior to January 2028, this will have no impact - thus the last physical games for PlayStation 5 will likely release in December 2027.

It is possible that we will see boutique publishers and printers of PlayStation 5 discs like we saw in the latter years of the PS Vita, and see with some indie games to this day that are doing smaller print runs. It also means that the PlayStation 6 will almost certainly release without any disc drive.

This comes after Sony stripped a bunch more purchased video licenses from PS5 users earlier this week, highlighting the transitory nature of digital media, even if it is highly convenient. This is going on at the same time as the Stop Killing Games movement is pushing for greater game preservation rights, and the ESA called community servers illegal.

PlayStation 3 and Vita Stores Closing 

Joining the removal of physical discs is the closure of the PlayStation 3 and PS Vita stores. This will start in August 2026 for Mexico, Hondoras, and Nicaragua, with Latin America countries for PlayStation 3 following in late 2026. For other countries, and the PS Vita store, they will shut down in July 2027, making you unable to buy new content purchasers after that date. Downloading from the servers will continue for the foreseeable future.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
